# Project Memory — test-memory
<!-- Generated by memory-core on 2026-05-11 — auto-sync keeps this fresh; manual refresh: memory-core sync -->

Architecture: Backend: NestJS
Stack: Typescript

## Retrieved Memories
_Pulled from central memory via semantic search on "Backend: NestJS Typescript"_

### 0. [rule] Services own all business logic
Every business rule, calculation, and orchestration lives in a service. Services are @Injectable() and framework-agnostic — they never import @nestjs/common HTTP decorators or Express/Fastify types.
_Tags: services, nestjs_


### 1. [rule] EventEmitter for in-process side effects
Use @nestjs/event-emitter to decouple side effects (send email, invalidate cache, log analytics) from the main business operation. Emit events from services, handle them in dedicated listener classes.
_Tags: events, async, nestjs_


### 2. [rule] ConfigModule with schema validation at startup
Use ConfigModule.forRoot() with a Joi or zod validation schema. The app must fail to start if required env vars are missing or have the wrong type. Never access process.env directly anywhere in the app.
_Tags: config, environment, nestjs_


### 3. [pattern] Use forRootAsync for config-dependent modules
Dynamic modules (database, cache) use forRoot() for static config and forRootAsync() when config depends on other providers like ConfigService.
_Tags: modules, config, nestjs_


### 4. [rule] API versioning
Version all public APIs from day one via URL prefix (/v1/) or header. Never make breaking changes to existing versions.
_Tags: api, versioning_


### 5. [rule] Dependency updates on a schedule
Review and update dependencies at least monthly. Use automated tools (Dependabot, Renovate) to get PRs for new versions. Never ignore security advisories.
_Tags: security, dependencies, devops_


### 6. [rule] Never expose internal IDs in public APIs
Use UUIDs or slugs as public identifiers in API URLs and responses. Never expose sequential database integer IDs to clients.
_Tags: security, api, privacy_


### 7. [rule] Sanitize user-generated content
Sanitize all user-generated content before rendering. Escape HTML, strip scripts, and use CSP headers to prevent XSS.
_Tags: security, xss, sanitization_


### 8. [rule] Typed config with registerAs namespaces
Group related config using registerAs(&quot;database&quot;, () &#x3D;&gt; ({ url: process.env.DATABASE_URL })). Inject with @InjectConfig(&quot;database&quot;) for typed access. Never use magic strings to access config keys.
_Tags: config, typescript, nestjs_


### 9. [rule] Throw NestJS exceptions from services
Services throw NotFoundException, ForbiddenException, BadRequestException, ConflictException — not raw Error objects. The global exception filter catches and formats them with the correct HTTP status automatically.
_Tags: exceptions, services, nestjs_


### 10. [rule] Migrations for all schema changes — never synchronize
Never use synchronize: true in any environment other than a throw-away local database. All schema changes are made through migrations generated via TypeORM CLI and committed to source control.
_Tags: database, migrations, nestjs_


### 11. [rule] Soft deletes
Use soft deletes (deleted_at timestamp) for user-generated data. Hard delete only for PII removal on request.
_Tags: database, soft-delete_


### 12. [rule] Correlation IDs
Attach a unique correlation/trace ID to every request. Propagate it through all downstream calls. Include it in logs and error responses.
_Tags: observability, tracing, api_


### 13. [rule] DTOs for API responses
Use DTOs for all API responses. Never expose raw database models to the client.
_Tags: api, dto, response_


### 14. [pattern] PartialType for update DTOs
Extend CreateUserDto with PartialType(CreateUserDto) to create UpdateUserDto. All fields become optional automatically with no duplication of validators.
_Tags: dto, nestjs_


### 15. [rule] Never return entities directly from controllers
Controller methods never return TypeORM/Prisma entities. Always map to a response DTO before returning. Use plainToInstance(ResponseDto, entity) or a dedicated mapper class.
_Tags: dto, security, entities, nestjs_
